Details:
Ultraviolet Irradiation  and Hydrogen Peroxide were  effective in removal of diuron from different types of water for the conditions examined.  Increase in hydrogen peroxide dosage increased the removal percentage.
Ferrous ions also act as a catalyst to the Ultraviolet Irradiation  and Hydrogen Peroxide reaction to increase diuron removal through the photo-Fenton reaction. At a constant diuron concentration, percent diuron removal increased with increasing hydrogen peroxide and ferrous ions concentrations up to a certain level above which diuron removal decreased due to scavenging effects of hydrogen peroxide on hydroxl radicals [782].
